Writing to outline cons, or disadvantages, involves focusing on the potential drawbacks or negative aspects of a situation, decision, or proposal. Here’s a general structure you can follow:

1. **Introduction**: Briefly introduce the context of the discussion and explain why it's important to consider the cons.

2. **List of Cons**: 
 - **Con 1**: Start with the most significant downside, explaining it clearly.
 - **Con 2**: Follow up with other disadvantages, providing examples or scenarios where they could be problematic.
 - **Con 3**: Continue listing other relevant cons, ensuring each point is detailed enough for the reader to understand the potential impact.

3. **Considerations**: Discuss any mitigating factors or ways to address the cons, if applicable.

4. **Conclusion**: Summarize the cons and re-emphasize why they should be taken seriously in decision-making.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Good place for people who want peaceful life, a govt job with mediocre salary without work.
2. Work life balance depends completely on you. You can work if you want, and don't work if you don't want.
3. Career progression can be fast if you are good in building relationships and influencing people at higher management.
4. Medical insurance facility and work environment is good and pretty safe.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Bad place for people who have desire to work hard, grow and achieve something great.
2. Performance assessment is heavily governed by personal biases and number of hour you spend in office rather than work.
3. Management not able to retain key talented employees. 
4. Work load share is skewed. Company earns 80% of its revenue because of work done by 10% of its employees, rest 90% are just liabilities. If you think you would be in that 10%, then it's not the place for you.
5. Most of the higher management
designates are incapable in rational decision making, and end up making decisions as per their personal benefits and biases rather than thinking what is good for companies growth.
6. Minimal freedom of speech.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Growth: Luck by chance. No one is there to guide you /assist you on technical growth. The company does not conduct an interview for a promotion so If you make your senior happy ( not by doing wonders in the technical domain) then yes you will be promoted on time. 
2. No vision: Projects are getting allocated on the mercy on defense ministry. So the moment it becomes private it won't be having any projects
3. Politics: Department is run by politics, either based on region or based on campus selected people or based on help
4. No value of talent. The only thing which works is your buttering skills. 
5. Medical policy: Yes hospitals exist near to BEL office but people prefer to go for taking referral ( So that they can visit the private hospital, very few doctors are good in the hospital). And medical policy is applicable only in Bangalore. If a person met an accident outside Bangalore then he /she should bear all expenses. 
6. Quarters: If you leave in quarters then you won't get HRA which is 27% of your basic and on top of that you need to pay tax.
7. HR department: Never seen them doing any HR activity.
8. Cadre basis food serving. Even the spoon is given based on the employee level.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
-Nothing technical is being done here. This company is a glorified procurement agency that buys all the parts from Indian or foreign vendors, re-brands it and sells it to government institutions because our government institutions are not allowed to directly procure material.
-no/ very low growth potential, since the work being done here does not compare favorably to the work being done outside in private firms in terms of quality, type and volume of assignments/projects.
-very restrictive work environment where phones and bags are not allowed, but the permanent employees still carry phones and because they outrank the security . The contractual positions are very harsh to work, because the BEL security abuse their power.
-Irrelevant postings and complete negligence from the HR . During the recruitment process, they conducted a technical interview to know about our strengths and weaknesses. During the posting of departments, they threw out the results of the interview out the window and randomly assigned departments. When you approach the HR for a department change they keep avoiding the situation and blame the employee for not knowing better. Getting a somewhat relevant department is like playing a game of Russian roulette with all but one bullet loaded.
-Most of the work , regardless of the department involves redundant documentation procedures. Even in Design and Engineering (R&D)Departments, majority of the work involves documentation

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Your job definition can change any time, sometimes it can change within hours.
2. No training and can be asked to do the floor-boy or accounts or courier job many times.
3. In the name of Transfer of technology, you have to just keep track of the components count.
4. You can't plan for long term growth as you don't know what next hour you will be asked to do.
5. All the greenery and infrastructure looks good but is very inefficient.
6. Salaries are very less and increments (3%) are too less, and yearly bonus is around 0-10,000 based on company performance which is consistently in losing profits and will maintain the legacy.
7. Company doesn't have any values and will lie with you on any matter from salary to capability etc. so be cautious, they are the mountebanks of the field.
8. Those who say there is lot to learn, My request to them to try to get a job outside on their valuable experience. I have seen manager who shamelessly accepts "Newly passed out students should not ask doubts to us as they are much more knowledgeable. ", This company will one day prepare you to be one of them
9. BEL does most of the time testing job only, most of the modules are outsourced to outside small companies. This is very painful when people less qualified than you get better job with your money.
10. In competition less market also it is struggling to perform, they recruit from top colleges or from all India exam. Which is cream in our society and BEL flush them.
11. It might be good for only those who in their life had never worked in a good lab environment and have very limited knowledge about whats happening in their field.
